expectations.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases
expectations.evidence: No coverage matrix, traceability table, or structured mapping of design elements to test cases exists anywhere in the file. The file contains only test code organized in @Nested classes with no matrix or mapping artifact.

expectations.text: Every sequential behavior step's on_failure path has a dedicated test (invalid_sync, unsupported_version, length_mismatch, checksum_failed, unknown_type)
expectations.evidence: invalid_sync: tested at lines 109, 119, 176, 189, 202. unsupported_version: tested at lines 239, 251, 264. length_mismatch: tested at lines 138, 298, 314. checksum_failed: tested at lines 351, 367. unknown_type: tested at line 401. All five on_failure paths have dedicated tests asserting the specific ErrorCode.

expectations.text: Every error_handling entry (null/empty raw_data, too-short raw_data) has a dedicated test
expectations.evidence: null input: line 109 (nullInput_returnsInvalidSync). empty input: line 119 (emptyInput_returnsInvalidSync). too-short input: line 138 (tooShort_returnsLengthMismatch, uses 11-byte array below the 12-byte minimum). All three error_handling entries are covered.

expectations.text: Boundary values tested for raw_data length (4 bytes minimum, 1024 bytes maximum, 3 bytes too short, 1025 bytes too long)
expectations.evidence: 4 bytes: tested at line 577 (fourBytes_belowMinimumFrame_returnsError). 1024 bytes: tested at line 589 (maxInputSize_withValidFraming_isAccepted). However, there is no test for exactly 3 bytes (too short boundary) and no test for 1025 bytes (too long boundary). The too-short tests use 11 bytes (line 138) and 1 byte (line 570), missing the specific 3-byte boundary. The over-max boundary of 1025 bytes is entirely absent.

expectations.text: Union return type handled: tests assert on specific Success or Error variant fields, not just non-null
expectations.evidence: Tests consistently call result.isError()/result.isSuccess() to discriminate the union, then access variant-specific fields: result.getError().getErrorCode(), result.getError().getByteOffset() for Error; result.getSuccess().getMessageType(), result.getSuccess().getSequenceNumber(), result.getSuccess().getPayload() for Success. Assertions check specific field values (e.g., assertEquals(ErrorCode.INVALID_SYNC, ...), assertEquals(messageType & 0xFF, ...)).

expectations.text: No test uses only 'assertDoesNotThrow' or 'assertNotNull' as its sole assertion
expectations.evidence: assertDoesNotThrow is never used in the file. assertNotNull appears only once at line 453 (validMessage_returnsPayloadMap), but that test also contains assertTrue(result.isSuccess()) at line 452, so assertNotNull is not the sole assertion. Every other test uses assertEquals, assertTrue, or assertNotEquals with substantive checks.

expectations.text: Test names describe the scenario being tested, not just the method name
expectations.evidence: All test method names follow a scenario_expectedOutcome pattern: nullInput_returnsInvalidSync, wrongFirstSyncByte_returnsInvalidSync, versionZero_returnsUnsupportedVersion, corruptedChecksum_returnsChecksumFailed, validMessage_returnsCorrectMessageType, errorThenSuccess_noStateLeakage, etc. Additionally, @DisplayName annotations provide human-readable descriptions. No test is named after just the method under test.

expectations.text: Happy path test: valid message parsed correctly with verified payload fields
expectations.evidence: validMessage_returnsCorrectMessageType (line 423): builds message with type 0x01 and seq 42, asserts result.isSuccess() and assertEquals(messageType & 0xFF, result.getSuccess().getMessageType()). validMessage_returnsCorrectSequenceNumber (line 435): asserts assertEquals(12345, result.getSuccess().getSequenceNumber()). zeroLengthPayload_producesEmptyMap (line 483): asserts payload.isEmpty(). Parsed fields (messageType, sequenceNumber) are verified against known input values. However, non-empty payload map contents are only checked for non-null (line 453), not for specific field values within the map.
